task_completion.description: Navigate to the Empire State Building in the 3D view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main viewport shows the Empire State Building (distinctive Art Deco skyscraper with a needle antenna) in New York City.

task_completion.description: Open the 3D Ruler or Measurement tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Ruler' dialog box is open, or the cursor indicates measurement mode (e.g., crosshairs or a specific icon).

task_completion.description: Measure the height of the building using the tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A measurement line (often yellow) is visible stretching from the ground/base to the roof or antenna tip of the building, or the Ruler dialog displays a length value between 370m and 455m.

task_completion.description: Open the dialog to create a new placemark.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'New Placemark' or 'Google Earth - New Placemark' properties window is open.

task_completion.description: Name the placemark 'Empire State Building Height'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Placemark properties window, the 'Name' field contains the text 'Empire State Building Height'.

task_completion.description: Document the measured height in the placemark description.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Placemark properties window, the 'Description' field contains text indicating the height (e.g., 'Measured height: 440 meters'). The value should be reasonably close to the actual height (381m-443m).

task_completion.description: Save the placemark to the Places sidebar.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A placemark labeled 'Empire State Building Height' is visible in the 'Places' sidebar (under My Places or Temporary Places).

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the application GUI and not terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Google Earth interface (menus, dialogs, 3D view) rather than a terminal or code editor scripting the KML.

integrity.description: Verify that the height was obtained via the measurement tool, not external search.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Ruler/Measurement tool was active and used on the building model. There should be no evidence of a browser search for 'Empire State Building height' being the sole source of the data.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the mapping software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The primary application in focus is the 3D mapping software (e.g., Google Earth Pro).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Empire State Building is located at 40.7484°N, 73.9857°W. Its roof height is 381 meters, and the total height to the antenna tip is 443 meters. A valid measurement using the 3D ruler should fall between approximately 370 meters and 455 meters.
